title: EXAMINATION: PATCHOULI AMBER (W01888276)
text:
OPERATIONS DEPARTMENT
M123
Ex officio refusal of protection (Article 7, Article 42(2), Article 119(2), Article 120(1),
Article 193(6) EUTMR)
Alicante, 19/03/2026
ISLAND IMAGINATION, LLC
2834 GULFGATE DR.
SARASOTA FL 34231
United States
Your reference: A0160961 87229887 5168841
International registration No: 1888276
Trade Mark: PATCHOULI AMBER
Name of the holder: ISLAND IMAGINATION, LLC
2834 GULFGATE DR.
SARASOTA FL 34231
United States
I. Summary of the facts
On 22/12/2025 the Office issued a Notification of ex officio provisional refusal of protection
pursuant to Article 7(1)(b) and (c) and Article 7(2) EUTMR because it found that the trade
mark applied for is descriptive and devoid of any distinctive character.
The goods for which the provisional refusal was issued were:
Class 3 Fragrant wax for use in potpourri burners.
Class 4 Candles.
Class 5 Deodorizer other than for personal use, namely, odor exterminating air and
room deodorizers for autos, homes, interiors, fabrics, upholstery, carpets, air,
vehicles, boats, motor homes, campers, households, offices, buildings,
hotels and motels.
The reasons are set out in the Notification of ex officio provisional refusal of protection,
which forms an integral part of this decision. It is accessible in the Annex of this decision.
In addition, the holder was requested to appoint a representative entitled to represent third
parties before the Office, pursuant to Articles 119(2) and 120(1) EUTMR.
II. Summary of the holder’s arguments
The holder failed to submit observations within the time limit.
III. Reasons
Avenida de Europa, 4 • E - 03008 • Alicante, Spain
Tel. +34 965139100 • www.euipo.europa.eu

Page 2 of 2
Pursuant to Article 94 EUTMR, it is up to the Office to take a decision based on reasons or
evidence on which the holder has had an opportunity to present its comments.
Having received no observations from the holder, both regarding the absolute grounds for
refusal and the appointment of a representative, the Office has decided to maintain the
grounds for refusal set out in the Notification of ex officio provisional refusal of protection.
IV. Conclusion
For the abovementioned reasons, and pursuant to Article 7(1)(b) and (c) and Article 7(2)
EUTMR, protection of the international registration No 1888276 is refused for the European
Union.
According to Article 67 EUTMR, you have a right to appeal against this decision. According
to Article 68 EUTMR, notice of appeal must be filed in writing at the Office within two months
of the date of notification of this decision. It must be filed in the language of the proceedings
in which the decision subject to appeal was taken. Furthermore, a written statement of the
grounds of appeal must be filed within four months of the same date. The notice of appeal
will be deemed to be filed only when the appeal fee of EUR 720 has been paid.
Judit CSENKE

OPERATIONS DEPARTMENT
W110
Notification of ex officio provisional total refusal of protection
(Article 5 of the Madrid Protocol, Rule 17(1) and (2) of the Regulations under
the Protocol relating to the Madrid Agreement, and Article 33 EUTMIR)
Alicante, 19/12/2025
BEGINNING OF TIME LIMIT: 19/12/2025
END OF TIME LIMIT: 19/02/2026
International registration number: 1888276
Trade mark: PATCHOULI AMBER
Name of the holder: ISLAND IMAGINATION, LLC
Protection of the abovementioned mark is provisionally refused for the European Union for
all the goods covered by the designation of the European Union.
I. Grounds
The Office has examined your international registration designating the European Union to
ensure that it does not fall under any of the grounds for refusal established under Article 7
EUTMR.
The sign
The application consists of the word mark ‘PATCHOULI AMBER’.
Legal basis for the objection
Article 7(1)(b) and (c) EUTMR
The sign you have applied for is ineligible for registration under Article 7(1)(b) and (c) and
Article 7(2) EUTMR because it describes certain characteristics of the goods for which
protection is sought and is devoid of any distinctive character.
The goods for which this objection is raised are:
Class 3 Fragrant wax for use in potpourri burners.
Class 4 Candles.
Avenida de Europa, 4 • E - 03008 • Alicante, Spain
Tel. +34 965139100 • www.euipo.europa.eu

Page 2 of 7
Class 5 Deodorizer other than for personal use, namely, odor exterminating air and
room deodorizers for autos, homes, interiors, fabrics, upholstery, carpets,
air, vehicles, boats, motor homes, campers, households, offices, buildings,
hotels and motels.
Descriptiveness
How descriptiveness is assessed depends on how the relevant consumer would perceive
the sign in relation to the goods and services for which protection is sought. In this case, the
relevant English-speaking consumer would understand the sign as having the following
meaning: perfume made of patchouli oil and amber fragrance.
The abovementioned meaning of the words ‘PATCHOULI AMBER’, of which the trade mark
consists, is supported by the following dictionary references.
PATCHOULI ‘an East Indian mint (Pogostemon cablin) that yields a heavy, dark-brown,
fragrant oil’
‘a perfume made from this oil’
(information extracted from Collins English Dictionary on 19/12/2025 at
https://www.collinsdictionary.com/dictionary/english/patchouli).
AMBER ‘a yellow or yellowish-brown hard translucent fossil resin derived from
extinct coniferous trees that occurs in Tertiary deposits and often contains
trapped insects . It is used for jewellery , ornaments , etc’
‘a medium to dark brownish-yellow colour, often somewhat orange , similar
to that of the resin’
(information extracted from Collins English Dictionary on 19/12/2025 at
https://www.collinsdictionary.com/dictionary/english/amber).
Besides the above meaning of being dark brownish-yellow colour, ‘AMBER’ also designates
a scent as follows:

Page 3 of 7
https://www.perfumesloewe.com/es/en_ES/all-perfumes/amber-perfumes/
https://www.perfumelounge.eu/fragrance-families/amber
https://50-ml.eu/fragrance/ingredient/amber

Page 4 of 7
https://papaduk.com/en/olfactory-families/ambar/
The relevant consumers would perceive the sign as providing information that the objected
fragrant wax (Class 3), candles (Class 4) and deodorizers (Class 5) feature the scent of
patchouli and amber or contain these as ingredients. Therefore, the sign describes the
ingredients and/or the scent of the goods or conceivably a combination of these
characteristics (e.g., both the product's fragrance and ingredient).
Lack of distinctiveness
Given that the sign has a clear descriptive meaning, it is also devoid of any distinctive
character and therefore ineligible for registration under Article 7(1)(b) EUTMR. This means
that it is incapable of performing the essential function of a trade mark, which is to
distinguish the goods or services of one undertaking from those of other undertakings.
Furthermore, signs that are commonly used in connection with the marketing of the goods
concerned are devoid of distinctive character for those goods.
In this context, an internet search dated 19/12/2025 revealed that the words ‘PATCHOULI
AMBER’ are commonly used in the relevant market:

Page 5 of 7
https://oaklanecandleco.com/products/patchouli-amber
https://oliveacandle.com/products/scented-hand-poured-soy-candle-amber-patchoulli

Page 6 of 7
https://candleworld.eu/en/products/fragrance-sticks-bispol-80-ml-patchouli-amber-7974.html
Consequently, taken as a whole, the sign is descriptive and devoid of distinctive character. It
is therefore incapable of distinguishing the goods for which an objection has been raised
under Article 7(1)(b) and (c) and Article 7(2) EUTMR.
II. Representation
The holder of the international registration is obliged to be represented before the Office by a
legal practitioner or professional representative that is entitled to represent third parties
before the EUIPO (Articles 119(2) and 120(1) EUTMR). Protection of the international
registration for the European Union will be refused in whole if a representative is not
appointed within the time limit indicated below.
III. Time Limit
The holder of the international registration is hereby given a time limit of two months to
overcome the grounds for refusal indicated under point I above and to comply with the
requirements indicated under point II above. This time limit will start to run on the day the
Office issues this notification (Article 193(2), (3) and (4) EUTMR). Any reply to this
communication must be addressed to the EUIPO only.
If you do not send any reply within the time limit, the Office will issue an appealable decision
refusing the protection of the international registration for the European Union.
If you need additional explanation on any aspect of this communication, please contact the
EUIPO’s Information Centre on +34 965139100 making reference to your application
number. The Information Centre will provide an answer to your question, or will put you
through to the examiner in charge of the file. If the examiner is not available, you may
request a ‘call back’ and the examiner will contact you within two working days.

Page 7 of 7
Judit CSENKE
Examiner
